response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but the role requires deep, sustained C++ expertise for industrial automation — and that is precisely what is absent. C++ does not appear in his skills section at all; his only relevant exposure is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis. There is no evidence of industrial automation, MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle experience anywhere on the resume. His strengths in TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven web systems do not compensate for the core technical gap this role demands.
strengths: Broad distributed-systems and event-driven architecture experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby).
Demonstrated agile craftsmanship — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — and a track record of mentoring through workshops and code katas.
Familiarity with React and Node.js, which are listed as advantageous skills in the JD.
concerns: C++ is absent from his skills list and amounts to roughly 6 months of exposure a decade ago — far below the 8–12 years of hands-on C++ required.
Zero industrial automation, IoT, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0).
No mention of Secure Development Lifecycle, IEC 62443, or any industrial cybersecurity practice — a core, non-negotiable requirement of the role.
